root_text.sn48.55:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ye keci sāragandhā, lohitacandanaṁ tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.sn48.55:1.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, ye keci bodhipakkhiyā dhammā, paññindriyaṁ tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati, yadidaṁ—bodhāya.
root_text.sn48.55:1.4: Saddhindriyaṁ, bhikkhave, bodhipakkhiyo dhammo, taṁ bodhāya saṁvattati.
root_text.sn48.55:1.8: paññindriyaṁ bodhipakkhiyo dhammo, taṁ bodhāya saṁvattati.
root_text.sn48.55:1.9: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ye keci sāragandhā, lohitacandanaṁ tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati;
root_text.sn48.55:1.10: evameva kho, bhikkhave, ye keci bodhipakkhiyā dhammā, paññindriyaṁ tesaṁ aggamakkhāyati, yadidaṁ—bodhāyā”ti.

translation_text.sn48.55:1.1: “Of all kinds of fragrant heartwood, red sandalwood is said to be the best.
translation_text.sn48.55:1.2: In the same way, the faculty of wisdom is said to be the best of the qualities that lead to awakening in terms of becoming awakened.
translation_text.sn48.55:1.3: And what are the qualities that lead to awakening?
translation_text.sn48.55:1.8: and wisdom are qualities that lead to awakening, in that they lead to becoming awakened.
translation_text.sn48.55:1.9: Of all kinds of fragrant heartwood, red sandalwood is said to be the best.
translation_text.sn48.55:1.10: In the same way, the faculty of wisdom is said to be the best of the qualities that lead to awakening in terms of becoming awakened.”
